preamble: Vocalization of [v] can result in the mid-back rounded vowel [o], particularly through the simplification of diphthongs created by an earlier vocalization stage or through the merger of labial glides with back vowels.

examples.note: The labial glide (historically related to v) merged into the mid-vowel.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
